Container.Name: jotty

Branch.TagDescription: Latest development branch commits. Used for development and testing of the latest features

Container.Overview: jotty-page is a lightweight alternative for managing your personal checklists and notes. It's extremely easy to deploy, keeps all your data on your own server and allows you to encrypt/decrypt your notes for your personal peace of mind.
Container.Requires: Don't forget to click **Show more settings...** for all **Variables**!

Config.Name: PORT
Config.Description: You can change 1122 to any available port on your host system.

Config.Name: NODE_ENV
Config.Description: Sets the Node.js environment to production mode for optimal performance and security.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: Process User ID that the container will run as.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Process Group ID that the container will run as.

Config.Name: UMASK
Config.Description: Sets the default file creation mask.

Config.Name: HTTPS
Config.Description: Enables HTTPS mode for secure connections.

Config.Name: App URL
Config.Description: Base URL of your jottyÂ·page instance. Required for secure session (https) and SSO. Include the https://

Config.Name: Serve Public Images
Config.Description: Allows public access to uploaded images via direct URLs.

Config.Name: Serve Public Videos
Config.Description: Allows public access to uploaded files via direct URLs.

Config.Name: Serve Public Files
Config.Description: Allows public access to uploaded files via direct URLs.

Config.Name: Stop Check Updates
Config.Description: If set to yes stops the github api call and won't give you a toast when a new update is available.

Config.Name: Disable Bruteforce Protection
Config.Description: Optional. Disables brute force protection for local login authentication. By default, accounts are temporarily locked after 3 failed login attempts with exponential delays (10s, 30s, 60s, etc.). Set to true to completely disable this security feature.

Config.Name: Default Locale
Config.Description: Optional. Sets the default language for the application (e.g., on the login page) when no user is logged in or a user hasn't set a preference.

Config.Name: Enable PWA Zoom
Config.Description: Optional. Enables zooming on the PWA for accessibility reasons.

Config.Name: Auth Mode
Config.Description: Enables OIDC (OpenID Connect) single sign-on or LDAP/Active Directory authentication. Set to 'oidc' or 'ldap'. Supersedes SSO_MODE.

Config.Name: SSO Mode (legacy)
Config.Description: Legacy fallback for AUTH_MODE. Kept for backward compatibility with existing setups — prefer AUTH_MODE for new installs. Leave empty if AUTH_MODE is set.

Config.Name: OIDC Issuer
Config.Description: URL of your OIDC provider (e.g., Authentik, Auth0, Keycloak).

Config.Name: OIDC Client ID
Config.Description: Client ID from your OIDC provider configuration.

Config.Name: OIDC Client Secret
Config.Description: Optional. Client secret for confidential OIDC client authentication.

Config.Name: OIDC Client ID File
Config.Description: Optional. Path to file containing the OIDC client ID. If set, takes priority over OIDC_CLIENT_ID. Useful for Docker Secrets.

Config.Name: OIDC Client Secret File
Config.Description: Optional. Path to file containing the OIDC client secret. If set, takes priority over OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET. Useful for Docker Secrets.

Config.Name: Local Auth Fallback
Config.Description: Optional. Allows both SSO and local authentication methods.

Config.Name: OIDC Admin Groups
Config.Description: Optional. Comma-separated list of OIDC groups that should have admin privileges.

Config.Name: OIDC Admin Roles
Config.Description: Optional. Comma-separated list of OIDC roles that should have admin privileges.

Config.Name: OIDC User Groups
Config.Description: Optional. Comma-separated list of OIDC groups allowed to access the application. If set, only users in these groups (or admins) can log in.

Config.Name: OIDC User Roles
Config.Description: Optional. Comma-separated list of OIDC roles allowed to access the application. If set, only users with these roles (or admins) can log in.

Config.Name: OIDC Groups Scope
Config.Description: Scope to request for groups. Defaults to 'groups'. Set to empty string or 'no' to disable for providers like Entra ID that don't support the groups scope.

Config.Name: OIDC Logout URL
Config.Description: Optional. Custom logout URL for global logout. Full URL to redirect to when logging out.

Config.Name: LDAP URL
Config.Description: URL of your LDAP server (e.g., ldap://ldap.example.com:389). Use ldaps:// on port 636 for TLS. Required when AUTH_MODE=ldap.

Config.Name: LDAP Bind DN
Config.Description: Distinguished name of the service account used to search the directory (e.g., cn=service,dc=example,dc=com). Required when AUTH_MODE=ldap.

Config.Name: LDAP Bind Password
Config.Description: Password of the LDAP service account. Required when AUTH_MODE=ldap (unless LDAP_BIND_PASSWORD_FILE is set).

Config.Name: LDAP Bind Password File
Config.Description: Optional. Path to a file containing the LDAP service account password. Takes priority over LDAP_BIND_PASSWORD. Useful for Docker Secrets.

Config.Name: LDAP Base DN
Config.Description: Base DN under which to search for users (e.g., ou=users,dc=example,dc=com). Required when AUTH_MODE=ldap.

Config.Name: LDAP User Attribute
Config.Description: Optional. The LDAP attribute matched against the submitted username. Defaults to 'uid'. Use 'sAMAccountName' for Active Directory.

Config.Name: LDAP Admin Groups
Config.Description: Optional. Pipe-separated list of group DNs granted admin rights on first login (e.g., cn=admins,ou=groups,dc=example,dc=com). Use '|' as separator since DNs contain commas. Requires memberof overlay on the LDAP server.

Config.Name: LDAP User Groups
Config.Description: Optional. Pipe-separated list of group DNs allowed to log in. Only members of these groups (or admin groups) can authenticate. Requires memberof overlay on the LDAP server.

Config.Name: Node Extra CA Certs
Config.Description: Optional. Path (inside the container) to a CA certificate file. Use this for LDAPS with a self-signed certificate (e.g., /app/config/ldap-ca.crt).

Config.Name: Internal API URL
Config.Description: Use if getting 403 errors after SSO login: Set to http://localhost:3000
